module_mood_contract: M5 mechanical rescue — 'Ahh, this is actually fixable.' Fabrication has signature shapes; the fix is mechanical: tighten the rules, regenerate, check again. But rescue, not triumph: the judge names its blind spots, M3's strategic uncertainty and M4's security residual still stand. Hand-off to M6 is hunger, not closure.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
Minimal lecturing before contrast-mood exercises.
Walk-away test — student leaves while agent works.
Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Debrief forcing functions ('touch only the Groundedness checks section,' 'read back the section verbatim,' 'confirm each rule is in the file,' 'name the two patterns this prompt enforced') are all inside the fenced Debrief prompt block. Body callout after the prompt ('Notice what this prompt insists on...') explains what the prompt did — this is optional recognition prose per rule 16 carve-out. Diagnostic: reading prompt-to-prompt only, the mood and teaching moment still land. PASS.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text.
Cross-module artefact contracts explicit at both ends.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Start Here names judges/groundedness-judge.md as the produced artifact. Next section confirms M6 picks it up. Strategy doc entry for M6 confirms path verbatim.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Between-module reading appears in two places.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Homework after Module 5 section correctly places Mollick reading. Second placement in evaluations.md Prework cannot be verified from this file alone.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Verify evaluations.md Prework includes 'Read Ethan Mollick, Garbage Can and Bitter Lesson.' If missing, add it.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named and honoured.
Epistemic mood not punitive mood.
Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea leads with empirical method selection ('you run several on your own output, score them against a 30-claim benchmark, and keep the winner') not with 'hallucination.' Key Concepts lead with 'Benchmarking as a pattern,' 'Empirical method selection over authority' — positive disciplines.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity — no front-running, cross-module sequencing correct.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy doc specifies five detectors (source triangulation, entailment, citation integrity, self-consistency, counter-evidence search) and names the magic bar as 'five methods, one corpus.' Module file consistently says four detectors throughout body, Key Concepts, Debrief prompt, and maintainer block. File is internally consistent at four but diverges from the strategy contract.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Align detector count with strategy doc (five) or update content-strategy.md to canonicalize four. Not a teaching-moment front-run — purely a count mismatch between file and strategy doc.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key Concepts surfaces: empirical method selection over authority, benchmarking as a portable pattern, narrow judge (discipline not omniscience), grounded as the discipline. Three-plus theme instances present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No placeholders inside fenced blocks.
Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lead-in: 'Ask Claude to review the session and update ./CLAUDE.md with groundedness operating rules.' Command verb, under 20 words.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block.
No markdown shout inside fenced prompt blocks.
Mood lands — all beats 8+/10.
rules_evaluated.evidence: All seven phase scores 8 or 9. Close score 8. No beat below 8. See mood_summary.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — M3 uncertainty and M4 residual kept standing.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Start Here: 'Your Module 4 residuals stay named, not solved.' Debrief prompt close: 'which claim shape you are LEAST confident you caught correctly.' Known limit: line on judge file enforces not-triumph. Close mood 8 — hunger, not closure.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands reliably across persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Scoreboard is the structural mechanism — it fills regardless of persona skill level. Even a fast operator who skips body prose still runs the exercise and sees the scoreboard at phase 2 (mood 9). Teaching moment does not rely on reading optional body callout.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — file honours the strategy doc's Big Idea framing.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy doc: center of gravity is GROUNDED as the discipline; benchmarking as the empirical method. File Big Idea leads with empirical selection discipline. Key Concepts first bullet: 'Benchmarking as a pattern.' Aligns.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — close hands off to M6's open hook.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Next: '30 claims, four detectors, one judge. Now imagine 300 claims, runs on every build, corrections feed back.' Strategy doc M6 open: 'You can spot ungrounded output by eye. But you won't always be there. What if you could automate what you just did?' Seam matches.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Ambiguous instruction — Homework still-uncertain.md file reference.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Homework line 84: 'Carry your own module-5/still-uncertain.md line into Module 6 prework.' Trace ambiguous_instructions confirms: the Debrief prompt does NOT create module-5/still-uncertain.md. The still-uncertain content lands in ./CLAUDE.md (the LEAST-confident answer from the Debrief summary). A student following this instruction literally searches for a file that does not exist.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Either (a) update the homework line to reference where the content actually lives ('the LEAST-confident line Claude named in your Debrief summary, now in ./CLAUDE.md'), or (b) add a Debrief step that saves the LEAST-confident claim to module-5/still-uncertain.md explicitly.
